key_findings: Four tool parsers defined (dnsx, httpx, nuclei, katana) with strict-then-quarantine design principle — malformed records are never silently coerced; all failures are quarantine events with reason codes
Schema version detection by field presence patterns (not version header) — tools don't emit formal version identifiers; field presence is the only reliable detection signal
dnsx v0/v1 migration: remap 'name' → 'host'; httpx v0/v1: remap 'input' → 'host'; nuclei v1/v2: remap 'templateID' → 'template-id'; katana v0/v1: wrap url in request object
Nuclei response/request fields MUST be truncated to 4KB at parse time — full raw HTTP can be 50KB+ per finding; at scale this exhausts Neo4j storage unnecessarily
Nuclei info-severity findings gated by KRIL score ≥ 50 — info findings are extremely high volume (technology disclosure); gating reduces noise by ~70% without losing high-value findings
NDJSON streaming parse (line-by-line) mandatory — tool output files can be 100MB per batch; loading full file into memory before parse exhausts Controller RAM
Single shared normalize_fqdn() and canonicalize_url() functions called by all parsers — prevents divergent normalization that creates dedup key mismatches across tools
Quarantine record format captures: original raw record, batch_id, chunk_id, parser_name, schema_version_detected, reason_code, quarantine_at — complete forensic record for all rejected data
ORS monitors quarantine_rate_pct (> 1% = CRITICAL) and quarantine_reason_code_new (first occurrence of new code = ALERT) — new codes indicate tool upgrade or output format change
Max line size: 1MB; lines exceeding are quarantined as OVERSIZED_RECORD — prevents parser memory exhaustion from malformed or intentionally large tool output lines

decisions.title: Strict-then-quarantine design
decisions.summary: Parser attempts full schema validation; any required field missing or malformed = quarantine with reason code; no silent coercion
decisions.rationale: Silent coercion produces corrupt ingest data that passes integrity checks but contains wrong values. Quarantine preserves forensic record and makes data quality issues visible.

decisions.title: Schema version by field presence patterns
decisions.summary: Version detected from field presence (host vs name, template-id vs templateID, etc.) — not from a version header field
decisions.rationale: None of the four tools emit a formal schema version field in their output. Field presence detection is universally applicable and forward-compatible (new fields don't break detection).

decisions.title: Nuclei response/request truncated to 4KB
decisions.summary: nuclei request and response fields truncated to 4KB at parse time; evidence_truncated=true flag stored
decisions.rationale: Full nuclei HTTP request/response can be 50KB+ per finding. At 50 findings/hour × 72h = 50MB of raw HTTP text in the graph. 4KB preserves sufficient forensic context for analyst review.

decisions.title: Nuclei info-severity KRIL gate
decisions.summary: Info-severity nuclei findings only ingested if KRIL score >= 50; configurable threshold; all other severities ingested unconditionally
decisions.rationale: Info findings (technology disclosure, version banners) are high-volume and low actionability for low-value targets. KRIL gate reduces noise by ~70% while preserving info findings for high-KRIL targets where tech stack matters.

decisions.title: NDJSON streaming parse (line-by-line)
decisions.summary: Parse tool output NDJSON one line at a time; never load full file into memory before starting parse
decisions.rationale: Tool output files can be 100MB per batch (nuclei at 25 concurrent × 5 min × full response body). Loading entire file into memory before parse exhausts 16GB Controller RAM during ingest.

decisions.title: Single shared normalization functions
decisions.summary: normalize_fqdn() and canonicalize_url() are shared library functions called by all four parsers — no inline normalization
decisions.rationale: Divergent normalization implementations across parsers produce different dedup keys for the same entity, creating phantom duplicates that bypass Bloom filter dedup.

known_quirks: NXDOMAIN records: set resolved=false; ingest as unresolved Subdomain
Empty arrays: absent array field = empty array (not missing required field)
Wildcard records: a_records matching wildcard IPs → DNS_WILDCARD_HIT quarantine
SERVFAIL: do NOT set resolved=false; queue for retry — infrastructure issue, not non-existence
body field: absent by default; present if --include-response-body flag used; max 50MB
content_length: -1 if Transfer-Encoding chunked; treat as unknown; do not quarantine
failed=true records: ingest as HTTPEndpoint with status_code=null; valid observation
Port in URL: preserve non-standard port in canonical URL
Redirect chain: deduplicate against url field to remove self-reference
url_normalization_steps: Strip tracking params (utm_source, utm_medium, fbclid, gclid, etc.)

known_quirks: request/response fields: truncate to 4KB; set evidence_truncated=true
info severity gate: only ingest if KRIL score >= 50; configurable threshold
Template path vs ID: use template-id not path as canonical identifier
Missing classification block: set cvss=null, cve_id=null; do not quarantine

entity_outputs: HTTPEndpoint: discovered URL as endpoint node linked to parent Subdomain
LINKS_TO relationship: cross-page links within same domain
technologies[]: stored on Subdomain node property
known_quirks: Binary content URLs: skip body; still ingest URL as HTTPEndpoint
Cross-origin links: flag out_of_scope=true; do not ingest as Subdomain
Duplicate URL discovery: dedup key handles idempotently via MERGE
JS-rendered URLs: strict query param canonicalization prevents dedup explosion

risk_model.title: Tool upgrade breaks schema silently
risk_model.mitigation: Version detection catches known transitions; unknown schemas quarantine; ORS alerts on new quarantine reason codes

risk_model.title: Nuclei response body size causes memory spike
risk_model.mitigation: Truncate to 4KB at parse time; never buffer full body

risk_model.title: URL canonicalization edge cases (IDN, encoded chars)
risk_model.mitigation: Use established URL library; comprehensive test vector suite

risk_model.title: katana JS-rendered URL dedup explosion
risk_model.mitigation: Strict query param canonicalization; session-scoped dedup cache within AWSEM chunk

risk_model.title: High quarantine rate masking systematic parser bug
risk_model.mitigation: ORS alert on quarantine_rate > 1%; first-occurrence alerting for new reason codes

recommended_actions.action: Implement single shared normalize_fqdn() and canonicalize_url() functions called by all four parsers
Use streaming NDJSON parse (line-by-line) — never load full file into memory
Add quarantine_reason_code ORS monitor with first-occurrence alerting for new codes
Truncate nuclei request/response to 4KB at parse time; store evidence_truncated=true flag
Gate nuclei info findings by KRIL >= 50; make threshold configurable in parser config
Run all 4 parser contracts against synthetic malformed inputs as pre-deployment test suite
Use established URL library (not custom) for canonicalization — IDN and percent-encoding have well-known edge cases

assumptions: A1: All four tools output NDJSON format; no multi-line JSON
A2: Tool output files are complete before parser starts; atomic file move from temp dir guarantees this
A3: NDJSON files may be up to 100MB per batch; streaming parse is required
A4: URL canonicalization library handles IDN correctly
A5: nuclei template tags are primary source for finding_class; taxonomy may evolve
A6: katana headless browser JS execution may produce dynamic query params; canonicalization must normalize these
A7: info severity gate threshold (KRIL >= 50) is configurable; default is conservative
